summary.intent: Add 100+ bundled default skills to Moltis (sourced from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw) so agents have rich capabilities out of the box, with category metadata, web UI browsing, and auto-install for missing CLI dependencies.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented a full bundled skills system: embedded 100 skills via include_dir!, added category metadata, web UI with category filters and inline detail view, format fallback for system prompt, auto-install hints on read_skill, and fixed the system prompt discovery path that was missing bundled skills entirely.

repo: Skills discovery for the system prompt happens in crates/chat/src/prompt.rs::discover_skills_if_enabled(), NOT in the gateway services — a separate code path from the web UI skill listing
The web UI skill listing uses /api/skills in crates/web/src/api.rs which had its own FsSkillDiscoverer, independent of gateway services.rs
Bundled skills are embedded at crates/skills/src/assets/ using include_dir! macro, mirroring the web UI assets pattern in crates/web/src/assets/
Skills priority order: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled — user skills always override bundled by name
The skill_tools.rs was refactored into a module directory (skill_tools/) on main branch during this PR, causing a merge conflict that required porting changes to read_ops.rs
YAML frontmatter with @ characters (scoped npm packages like @steipete/summarize) must be double-quoted to be valid YAML
Directory name must match frontmatter 'name' field — 6 skills had mismatches (e.g. trl-fine-tuning/ vs name: fine-tuning-with-trl) causing silent read failures
Auto-install via tokio::process::Command runs on the gateway host, NOT in the sandbox container where exec runs — fundamental environment mismatch
Biome formatter uses nightly-2025-11-30 toolchain and has strict indentation rules for multi-line arrow function bodies in JSX
check-file-size.sh needed exclusions for .moltis/ (installed skills) and crates/skills/src/assets/ (vendored sidecar files)

code.finding: SkillDiscoverer import was at top level but used inside cfg blocks that had their own local use statements — caused unused import warning
BundledSkillStore needs recursive directory traversal for mlops/ which has 3-level nesting (mlops/training/axolotl/SKILL.md)
CompositeSkillDiscoverer deduplication must use seen.insert() not !seen.contains() to also deduplicate within bundled set itself
Models send file_path: '' (empty string) on first read_skill call causing error — treat empty string same as null/absent
npm --ignore-scripts flag breaks scoped package installs (@steipete/summarize) in some npm versions
Biome requires specific indentation depth for multi-line arrow function bodies — body content needs one extra tab level vs closing brace
skill_detail handler had no branch for SkillSource::Bundled, causing clicks on bundled skills in web UI to silently fail

workflow: Auto-install of skill CLIs should provide install commands for the agent to run via exec (in sandbox), not execute them server-side on the gateway host
When porting skills from other repos, check directory name matches frontmatter name field — 6/101 skills had mismatches
YAML values starting with @ must be quoted: bins: ['@scope/package'] not bins: [@scope/package]
The godmode/red-teaming skill should not be bundled by default — security concern raised in Greptile review
Sidecar reference files (unsloth/references/llms-full.md at 16.8k lines, XSD schemas, LaTeX templates) account for ~144k of the 162k added lines
Greptile auto-review is valuable — caught path normalization missing in bundled sidecar reads and Disable button showing for read-only bundled skills
When merging main with module restructuring conflicts, accept main's structure entirely then re-apply changes rather than trying to merge line-by-line

friction: system prompt was missing all bundled skills for the entire session because discover_skills_if_enabled() in chat/src/prompt.rs was a completely separate code path from gateway services — not discovered until user reported agent couldn't find bundled skills
Auto-install ran on gateway host but exec runs in sandbox container — installs appeared to succeed but binary was never available to the agent
Biome formatter required multiple rounds of fixing (3 separate commits) due to indentation being off by exactly one tab level in arrow function bodies
sed-based origin metadata injection corrupted some SKILL.md files requiring a full re-copy from source repos
skill_tools.rs was refactored into a module directory on main branch during the PR, requiring a full re-port of changes to the new read_ops.rs location
read_skill returning error on file_path: '' caused every model call to fail once then retry with null — two round trips wasted per skill activation
npm EINVALIDPACKAGENAME error with @steipete/summarize was caused by --ignore-scripts flag interaction with scoped packages, not the @ character itself
open_items: plan skill still references .hermes/plans/ directory in its body — should be updated to Moltis equivalent path
google-workspace skill still references Hermes-managed OAuth2 setup — needs Moltis-specific OAuth instructions
Annotate ineligible skills in system prompt (e.g. <skill name='summarize' eligible='false'>) so agent knows before trying to use a skill with missing binary
youtube-content skill could replace summarize skill for YouTube use case — both exist but agent doesn't know to prefer youtube-content for YouTube URLs
obliteratus skill (removes refusal behaviors from LLMs) is bundled — may warrant security review similar to godmode that was removed
Skills from openclaw extensions/ directory (Feishu, QQBot platform-specific) were skipped — could be added with platform guards
Auto-install should detect sandbox vs host environment and route accordingly rather than just providing install hint text
